Package de.uka.ilkd.key.proof.init.loader

Interface ProofObligationLoader

All Known Implementing Classes:
DependencyContractPOLoader, FunctionalBlockContractPOLoader, FunctionalLoopContractPOLoader, FunctionOperationContractPOLoader, InfFlowContractPOLoader, ProgramMethodPOLoader, ProgramMethodSubsetPOLoader, TacletProofObligationInputLoader, WellDefinednessPOLoader
@NullMarked public interface ProofObligationLoader
Interface for writing the handling of the creation of proof obligations.

A proof obligation load takes the environment loaded from a key file, and instantiates this into the initial sequent (called proof obligation). ProofObligationLoader are loaded with the help of the ServiceLoader, hence you need to register them into the META-INF folder.

A ProofObligationLoader decides by itself whether it can handle a certain KeYUserProblemFile, by given the class entry from the file's \proofObligation configuration object.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    handles(String identifier)
    Receiving an identifier (traditionally the fully qualified class name), this method decides whether it can handle the current situation.
    IPersistablePO.LoadedPOContainer
    loadFrom(InitConfig initConfig, Configuration properties)
    Builds the PO from the given environment and \proofObligation configuration.

  • Method Details

    • loadFrom

      IPersistablePO.LoadedPOContainer loadFrom(InitConfig initConfig, Configuration properties) throws Exception
      Builds the PO from the given environment and \proofObligation configuration.
      Parameters:
      initConfig - the key environment
      properties - the \proofObligation configuration
      Returns:
      always a valid PO
      Throws:
      Exception - in case of an arbitrary exception, e.g., missing information properties

    • handles

      boolean handles(String identifier)
      Receiving an identifier (traditionally the fully qualified class name), this method decides whether it can handle the current situation. Currently, the identifier corresponds to the class entry in the \proofObligation entry in the KeYUserProblemFile.
      Parameters:
      identifier - non-null string
      Returns:
      true if this load handles this type of PO
